content.title: Poland parliament fails again to override presidential veto on crypto bill
content.summary: Poland’s parliament has once again failed to overturn President Karol Nawrocki’s veto of the crypto regulation bill.

content.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
content.summary: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, NFTs, Web3 and crypto regulation.

content.title: Stablecoins behave like FX markets as liquidity splits: Eco CEO
content.summary: Stablecoins promise seamless dollar movement, but fragmented liquidity is turning large transfers into complex execution problems, says Eco CEO Ryne Saxe.

content.title: Spot Bitcoin ETFs attract nearly $1B in weekly inflows as risk sentiment improves
content.summary: Spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded nearly $1 billion in weekly inflows, their strongest performance in over three months amid improving risk sentiment.

content.title: SEC charges Donald Basile in $16M crypto fraud tied to ‘insured’ token
content.summary: The SEC has sued crypto executive Donald Basile over an alleged $16 million scheme involving false claims about an “insured” Bitcoin Latinum token.

content.title: Circle unveils USDC Bridge for native cross-chain stablecoin transfers
content.summary: The USDC Bridge adds to Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol, which often sees over $500 million worth of USDC transfers each day.

content.title: X's new Cashtags feature drives $1B trading volume in first two days
content.summary: X’s Cashtags feature has been integrated into Canadian-based online brokerage Wealthsimple, but has not yet been adopted by a US trading platform.

content.title: Worldcoin tanks 13% as World’s iris-scanning tech expands to Zoom, Docusign
content.summary: Most of the integrations are aimed at fighting deepfakes as the rise of AI-generated content makes it increasingly difficult to distinguish humans from AI.

content.title: Russia introduces bill to criminalize unregistered crypto services
content.summary: Individuals and groups would be required to register with the Bank of Russia before offering certain crypto services, or potentially face fines and prison time.

content.title: Polymarket odds of Hormuz Strait traffic normalizing by end of May spike to 73%
content.summary: Traders on the Polymarket prediction market platform place the odds of the Strait returning to normal traffic by May 31, 2026, at 73%.
